questions: Should the home button appear on EVERY tool card (including the currently-default one, just rendered highlighted), or should it be hidden on the default and only shown on hover for non-defaults? The brief says 'highlighted for the current default', implying it's always visible — I'm assuming always-visible.

If a user has set a default tool that later becomes invisible in their environment (e.g., a tool limited by `environments` or a feature flag like character-animate's cloud-mode gate), should the redirect: (a) silently fall back to travel-between-images, (b) fall back AND reset the stored preference, or (c) still navigate there and let the page handle it? I'm assuming (a) — fall back without overwriting, so the preference survives if the gate later re-opens.

Should the 'assistant' tools also be eligible to be set as the default? The brief only requires video-editor promoted to main, but the home button on an assistant tool would still work. I'm assuming yes — the button is universal, which is simpler and matches the brief's 'home button on each tool entry' language.

success_criteria.criterion: `tsc --noEmit` (or the project's typecheck) passes with the new `defaultTool` key added to `UISettings` and the new `decodeDefaultTool` wired into `normalizeUserUISetting`.
`src/shared/lib/tooling/toolManifest.ts` sets `paneSection: 'main'` for the `videoEditorSettings` entry, and the tool appears under the 'Main Tools' section of `ToolsPane` at runtime.
Clicking the home button on a tool card calls `updateDefaultTool({ toolId })` WITHOUT navigating to that tool (click propagation stopped).
After setting a non-default tool as default and reloading the app at `/`, `/tools`, or post-login, the user lands on the tool whose id matches `users.settings.ui.defaultTool.toolId`.
The currently-default tool card renders the home icon in a visually distinct (filled/accented) state; all other cards render the home icon in a muted/outline state.
Users with no stored `defaultTool` preference still land on `travel-between-images` (backwards-compatible default).
If the stored `toolId` references a tool id missing from `toolRuntimeManifest`, the redirect falls back to `travel-between-images` and the preference is overwritten to the fallback.
No new Supabase migration file is added (the change reuses the existing JSONB `users.settings.ui` column).
Home button has `aria-label`, `type="button"`, and a tooltip; it is keyboard-focusable.
No other call sites hardcoding `/tools/travel-between-images` remain (outside of the `DefaultToolRedirect` fallback).
Manual smoke test: home button persists across page reloads and across browser sessions for the same user.
Manual smoke test: setting a default that is hidden by the character-animate cloud-mode gate falls back gracefully without crashing.

assumptions: The home button is rendered on every ToolCard (main + assistant sections), not just main tools. Clicking it sets that tool as default regardless of section.
Fallback tool id is 'travel-between-images' (preserves today's behavior for users who never set a preference).

If the stored `toolId` references a tool that no longer exists in `toolRuntimeManifest`, redirect to the fallback and overwrite the bad value. If it exists but is currently hidden by env/feature gate, redirect to the fallback WITHOUT overwriting (so preference survives gate changes).
No new Supabase migration is needed — `users.settings` is already JSONB and `useUserUIState` handles auto-backfill on first read.

The loader-to-component conversion for `/tools` (now rendering `<DefaultToolRedirect />` instead of a synchronous `redirect()`) is acceptable; the brief short ReighLoading flash during the Supabase fetch is fine (matches existing lazy-load UX).
No analytics/telemetry event needs to fire when the user changes their default tool.
The home-button icon uses lucide's `Home`, sized ~w-4 h-4, with `fill-current` when active and muted foreground otherwise. No new design token needed.
